_comment: All tunable Loop Protection thresholds in ONE place (spec Section 3). The watchdog READS these; it never rewrites them (a threshold change rides the repo + rollout, like all Loop Protection data). Every ceiling here is also a SAFETY CAP under Skill 60 Signal S4: a raise without an operator stamp is itself a P1 (spec 5.1/5.2).

tick._locked: T5/Section 3: every 15 minutes, jittered. Zero model calls; cadence costs CPU only. The system that hunts furnaces must be structurally incapable of being one.

burn_in._locked: T5: DRY_RUN observe-only for the first 7 days on any box; then Tier 1 arms. armed=false is the ledger default until the operator runs `arm`.

d1_restart_velocity._source: Box A ~56,050 restarts; Box F 4,528-in-10h. pm2 jlist restarts / launchctl runs / docker RestartCount, delta per unit per tick. NEVER an env dump (name/status/pid/restarts ONLY).

d2_token_burn_rate._source: Operator box ~11,800 calls/week; Box C ~10-12M input tokens/day idle. Paid vs local split, correlated with initiated-session presence (Skill 60 S5 method).

d3_identical_signature._source: Generalized loop-detector.sh progress test, made content-based: rolling hash over (error class + tool-call name sequence + target) in the new-bytes-since-last-tick slice.

d4_timer_refire._source: Cron fires > 2x declared cadence = P1; healthy-probe-but-no-progress 3 ticks = P1 wedge; any orphan listener on :18789 = P1 (Box E deferral deadlock).

backoff._locked: Section 5.2/5.4: exponential backoff with jitter is the ONLY sanctioned retry shape. Base 2h (the never-stop doctrine's own number), doubling, capped 24h, state persisted on disk per job (a backoff that lives in memory resets to zero on the next crash).

escalation._locked: Section 7: an unacknowledged P1 goes to Rescue Rangers after 30 minutes; a dead-man P1 escalates immediately. Thresholds shared with Skill 60.
